message.content:
You are assessing one Coachella Valley dining establishment for an honest category-map artifact. The artifact's job is to map who's there, who wins what, and where the visibility gaps are.

Entity: Rincón Norteño (Indio)
Cuisine/type signal: Family-owned legacy Mexican (Norteño)
Why it surfaced in discovery: Flores family restaurant since 1964; originally a 15-stool cafe next to the Desert Theater on Fargo Street; relocated 1979 to 83011 Indio Blvd; multi-generation Indio anchor, repeatedly cited by Visit Greater Palm Springs as a must-try Indio legacy spot.
Discovery sources: https://www.rinconnorteno.com/about | https://www.visitgreaterpalmsprings.com/listing/rincon-norte%c3%b1o-restaurant/7849/ | https://www.visitgreaterpalmsprings.com/blog/post/14-authentic-mexican-restaurants-in-greater-palm-springs/

Verify the entity exists, then assess on four dimensions. Use WebSearch (ToolSearch query "WebSearch") and WebFetch (ToolSearch query "WebFetch") to investigate. Read the entity's own website. Read 2-3 review-platform pages. Run regional queries to test visibility.

REPUTATION:
- review_volume — approximate Google/Yelp review count band (e.g., "3000+", "500-1000", "<100"). Mark [UNVERIFIED] if you can't find it.
- sentiment — current sentiment summary (positive / mostly positive / mixed / declining / negative). Recent reviews weighted over stale.
- recency_signal — what do reviews in the last 6-12 months say?
- longevity — years operating, ownership stability. Mark [UNVERIFIED] if unclear.
- credentials — earned third-party recognition (James Beard nominee/winner, Michelin, Food Network, Yelp Top 100, OpenTable Top, Desert Sun / Palm Springs Life awards, local press features). Empty array if none. Do NOT invent credentials.

AGENT_VISIBILITY:
- appears_in_queries — which regional queries actually surface this entity? Test specific queries: "best restaurants Coachella Valley", "fine dining Palm Desert", "[entity's cuisine] near Palm Springs", "date night Rancho Mirage", "best breakfast [city]".
- description_accuracy — when it surfaces, is the description correct?
- site_structured_data — fetch the entity's own website. Does it carry schema.org markup, clean hours, menu structure, location signals an agent can read?

VISIBILITY GAP — score 0-10. 0 = reputation and visibility aligned. 10 = high reputation, low/wrong visibility.

RELATIONSHIPS:
- competitors — 2-4 direct competitors in same city or cuisine bracket
- corridor_role — anchor / supporting / outlier. Major corridors: El Paseo (Palm Desert), Old Town La Quinta, downtown Palm Springs, The River (Rancho Mirage), festival-adjacent Indio, north Palm Desert development zone, uptown design district Palm Springs.
- query_wins — which queries it actually dominates

CONVERTIBILITY (the layer above the map):
- tier — "anchor-reference" (chain/franchise/resort-owned, no reachable local DM), "convertible" (independently owned, reachable, evidence of marketing spend), or "unclear"
- ownership_signal — independent / chain / franchise / resort-owned / hotel-restaurant / unclear
- marketing_spend_signal — what evidence of marketing budget? real site / paid listings / agency footprint / active social
- reasoning — one paragraph

sources_consulted: list URLs you actually consulted.
flags: anything you couldn't verify — list the unverified claim with [UNVERIFIED] tag.

Stay disciplined: do NOT invent awards, credentials, history, or specific stats. If you can't verify, flag it. The downstream verification layer will catch hallucinations.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- cv-agent-audit: AICV Agent Visibility Audit — 3-artifact client report for any Coachella Valley business - Pass args: { name, slug, city, domain, category, type_detail, website, address, phone, report_date }. Produces owner report, developer brief, and AICV node YAML seed.
- anthropic-skills:consolidate-memory: Reflective pass over your memory files — merge duplicates, fix stale facts, prune the index.
- anthropic-skills:docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:schedule: Create or update a scheduled task that runs automatically. Use when the user says things like "every day", "each morning", "remind me in an hour", "run this at noon", or wants to reschedule an existing task.
- anthropic-skills:setup-cowork: Guided Cowork setup — install role-matched plugins, connect your tools, try a skill.
- anthropic-skills:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- anthropic-skills:sunshine-fm-human-rhythm: Design music, jingles, and spoken word for SunshineFM using the Human Rhythm Engine. Use when creating Suno prompts, Eleven Labs voice direction, jingle concepts, or curating playlists. Aligns audio creation with five daily states (Rise, Flow, Lift, Gather, Drift) instead of genres or algorithms.
- anthropic-skills:xlsx: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.
- cowork-plugin-management:cowork-plugin-customizer: Customize a Claude Code plugin for a specific organization's tools and workflows. Use when: customize plugin, set up plugin, configure plugin, tailor plugin, adjust plugin settings, customize plugin connectors, customize plugin skill, tweak plugin, modify plugin configuration.
- cowork-plugin-management:create-cowork-plugin: Guide users through creating a new plugin from scratch in a cowork session. Use when users want to create a plugin, build a plugin, make a new plugin, develop a plugin, scaffold a plugin, start a plugin from scratch, or design a plugin. This skill requires Cowork mode with access to the outputs directory for delivering the final .plugin file.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

I found search results for Rincon Norteno in Indio. Here's what I found:

## Restaurant Overview
Rincón Norteño Restaurant, run by the Flores Family, has been serving authentic Mexican food for over 50 years. The restaurant is located at 83011 Indio Blvd, Indio, CA 92201 and is open daily from 7:00 AM - 9:00 PM.

## Services Offered
It's a full service Mexican restaurant serving breakfast, lunch, and dinner with full bar service including beer, wine, and liquor. Full service catering and take out are available.

## Reviews and Ratings
On Tripadvisor, Rincon Norteno Cafe is rated 4.2 of 5 based on 63 unbiased reviews and ranked #16 of 132 restaurants in Indio.

## Customer Feedback
Reviews highlight both positive and mixed experiences. The restaurant is known for being family-oriented with friendly staff and high level of service. The menu offers an array of options including tacos, ribeye, chilaquiles, and breakfast items alongside margaritas. Guests can enjoy a full bar and complimentary hot brown salsa.

For detailed reviews on specific platforms, you can visit their Yelp page or Google to see current customer feedback and ratings.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

# Rincón Norteño - Key Information Extract

**History & Founding:**
The restaurant has operated for over 50 years. Founders Prudencio (originally from Austin, Texas) and his wife Estella (from Santa Rita, New Mexico) relocated to Indio in 1959 and "started their family recipes with locals in 1964." The original location opened next to the old Desert Theater on Fargo Street with just 15 stools but attracted consistent weekend crowds.

**Expansion:**
In 1979, the business relocated to its current address at the corner of Fargo and Indio Boulevard due to growth.

**Family Ownership:**
The operation remains family-run, with Prudencio and Estella's children—Eloy, Gustavo, Heriberto, Rosa, and Prudencio Jr.—actively involved in managing the establishment as "a 'Family Affair'."

**Location(s):**
- Primary: 83011 Indio Blvd., Indio, CA 92201
- Service area: Coachella Valley region including Coachella, La Quinta, Palm Desert, Indian Wells, Rancho Mirage, Cathedral City, and Palm Springs

**Hours:**
Not specified in provided content.

**Menu Structure:**
Not detailed in provided content.

**Awards/Recognitions:**
Described as having "become a Coachella Valley legend" based on family tradition.

**Structured Data:**

Based on the search results, here are some of the best family-owned Mexican restaurants in Indio and the Coachella Valley with notable legacies:

## El Mexicali Café II

El Mexicali Café II has been serving up classic Mexican dishes for nearly 20 years and was named one of USA TODAY's 2026 Restaurants of the Year. The restaurant traces its heritage to Carmen Murguia, who started her family in the border town of Mexicali before moving to Delano, California, to open her first restaurant, later moving to the Coachella Valley. The original El Mexicali Café, near downtown Indio, is now owned by Monica Sandez, who recently took over from her retiring sister. House specialties include the Especial Del Mexicali — grilled skirt steak with sauteed cactus — and the famous shrimp-stuffed yellow peppers.

## Rincon Norteño

The Flores family started sharing their family recipes with Greater Palm Springs visitors and locals in 1964 when Rincon Norteño first opened, offering friendly service and authentic dine-in and catering options in Indio.

## Jalisco

Family owned and operated since 1980, Jalisco in Coachella remains one of Greater Palm Springs longest running restaurants. Local favorites include their birria soup and tortas.

## El Tranvia

El Tranvia first opened in 1969, and half a century later remains the area's go-to spot for delicious Mexican food.

content.content:
# Rincon Norteno Cafe - Key Information

**Review Count & Rating:**
- 63 total reviews
- 4.2 of 5 stars

**Ranking:**
#13 of 165 restaurants in Indio

**Recent Review Themes (Past 6-12 Months):**
- Authentic Mexican cuisine praised consistently
- "Great authentic food, but not for the person who loves ambience" - January 2025
- Strong salsa and chile relleno mentions
- Location noted as industrial/uninviting but food quality compensates

**Common Complaints:**
- Minimal atmosphere; described as "plain, no frills"
- Located in an area reviewers characterized as "shady" or "scary-looking"
- One recent review (Sept 2024) criticized tough, stringy beef in tacos

**Longevity & Awards:**
- Family-operated for 50 years
- No current Travelers' Choice award visible
- Described as having consistent staff and same cooks for 20+ years, suggesting established reputation

**Overall Pattern:**
Despite aesthetic shortcomings, the restaurant maintains strong loyalty due to authentic preparation and reasonable pricing. Recent feedback indicates this positioning remains unchanged.

# Rincon Norteno: Awards, Recognition & Legacy

**Year of Feature:** July 24, 2012

**Longevity/Legacy:**
The restaurant has been operating since 1964, establishing over four decades of continuous service to the Coachella Valley community.

**Unique Recognition:**
The establishment claims to be "the only Mexican restaurant in the valley that serves their salsa warm," distinguishing itself through this particular culinary practice.

**No family background, awards, or direct quotes about legacy were mentioned in the article.**

Based on my search results, here are some of the best authentic Mexican restaurants in the Coachella Valley and Indio area for 2025:

## Top Authentic Mexican Restaurants

**Jalisco Restaurant** - A family-owned Mexican eatery in Indio, California, that has been a favorite in the Coachella Valley for years. It's renowned for its authentic Mexican fare, offering an extensive menu featuring tacos, burritos, fajitas, seafood dishes, and vegetarian options.

**Pueblo Viejo Grill** - Established in 2005 in Indio, this restaurant has become reputable for its hospitality, fresh ingredients and exceptional cuisine, with an expansion to a Palm Desert location.

**El Tranvia** - This restaurant first opened in 1969 and remains the area's go-to spot for delicious Mexican food. The quesabirria tacos are exceptional, and other highlights include tacos gobernador (shrimp and cheese taco), costillas con nopales (pork ribs with cactus), and chicken mole.

**El Mexicali Cafe II** - A lively and vibrant cantina with a fully stocked bar and stylish decor that reflects the essence of Mexico, featuring outdoor patio seating.

**Mariscos El Capitan** - Located in Coachella, this family-owned spot is known for its authentic mariscos (Mexican seafood), from fresh ceviches and shrimp cocktails to whole fish and house specialties. It features live Banda music transforming dinner into a full-on night out, with live music every Thursday, Friday, and Sunday starting at 7 p.m.

**Rincon Norteño** - The Flores family started sharing their family recipes in Indio in 1964, and the friendly service paired with tasty and authentic dine-in and catering options make this place a must-try.

I've found search results for authentic Mexican breakfast in Indio, including information about menudo and chilaquiles. Here are the key findings:

## Authentic Mexican Breakfast in Indio

La Casa Del Mariachi, located in La Quinta, CA, offers a vibrant Mexican breakfast and brunch experience. Customers rave about the Menudo Soup, Asian Chicken Salad, and Chef's Salad, praising the fresh ingredients and flavorful combinations.

For a classic Indio breakfast with authentic Mexican breakfast dishes like chilaquiles, huevos rancheros, heuvos divorciados, and more, try Casa de Silvia.

## About the Dishes

**Chilaquiles:** Chilaquiles are one of the most iconic and traditional Mexican breakfasts. They are prepared with fried (or toasted) corn or tortilla chips and dipped in green or red chile sauce.

**Menudo:** The search results indicate that menudo is available at Mexican restaurants in Indio as part of their authentic breakfast offerings.

The search results show that Indio has several authentic Mexican breakfast restaurants that serve these traditional dishes, with particularly positive reviews for the quality and authenticity of their preparations.

content.text: I have enough verified data. Notable: Rincón Norteño does NOT surface in either of the two key regional queries I tested ("authentic Mexican breakfast Indio" and "best Mexican restaurant Indio Coachella Valley legacy") even though it serves chilaquiles, menudo, and dates to 1964. It only appears when explicitly featured in curated editorial lists (Visit Greater Palm Springs, Palm Springs Life round-ups). Let me submit.

input.name: Rincón Norteño
input.cuisine_or_type: Family-owned legacy Mexican (Norteño) — breakfast/lunch/dinner with full bar

reputation.review_volume: Yelp 278 reviews; Tripadvisor 63 reviews (#13 of 165 in Indio, 4.2/5); Birdeye 315 reviews (4.5); Restaurant Guru cites ~1,488 aggregated visitor reviews and a Google average of ~4.4 [UNVERIFIED — Google review count not directly confirmed]
reputation.sentiment: mostly positive — consistent praise for authenticity, warm salsa, generous portions, and family service; common caveat about plain/no-frills atmosphere and the surrounding industrial block
reputation.longevity: Founded 1964 by Prudencio and Estella Flores; relocated 1979 to 83011 Indio Blvd; still family-operated by the founders' children (Eloy, Gustavo, Heriberto, Rosa, Prudencio Jr.) — ~62 years continuous operation, multi-generational ownership stable
credentials: Featured in Visit Greater Palm Springs '14 Authentic Mexican Restaurants in Greater Palm Springs'
Featured in Palm Springs Life '9 Coachella Valley Restaurants for Authentic Mexican Cuisine'
Featured in Coachella Valley Weekly profile (July 2012)
reputation.recency_signal: Reviews from late 2024 through early 2025 echo the long-standing pattern — strong food (chilaquiles, huevos rancheros, chile relleno, warm salsa), staff continuity (same cooks 20+ years cited), with a Sept 2024 complaint about tough beef tacos and a Jan 2025 note that ambience is plain. No collapse in sentiment; reputation is steady.

agent_visibility.appears_in_queries: Surfaces only in curated editorial round-ups (Visit Greater Palm Springs, Palm Springs Life, Coachella Valley Weekly, Wanderlog list aggregations). Does NOT surface organically in tested generative-style queries: 'best Mexican restaurant Indio Coachella Valley legacy family-owned' returned El Mexicali Café II, Jalisco, El Tranvia, and only mentioned Rincón Norteño after the top picks; 'authentic Mexican breakfast Indio menudo chilaquiles' returned La Casa Del Mariachi and Casa de Silvia — Rincón Norteño was absent despite being a literal match. Effectively invisible outside of editorial citations.
agent_visibility.description_accuracy: When it does surface, descriptions are accurate but thin — 'Flores family since 1964, friendly service, authentic dine-in and catering.' No mention of the Fargo Street origin, the Desert Theater context, the 1979 relocation, or the multi-generational handoff. The richer narrative on the restaurant's own About page is not propagating.
agent_visibility.site_structured_data: rinconnorteno.com is a clean brochure site with the basics agents need in plain text (address 83011 Indio Blvd; phone 760-347-4754; 'OPEN DAILY 7am–9pm'; Menu/About/Press/Contact nav; Facebook/Instagram in footer) but NO JSON-LD schema.org markup, NO Open Graph tags, and the /press page is an empty shell. Limon Media credit in footer. Agents can extract NAP/hours from raw text but there is no Restaurant schema, no MenuSection markup, no openingHoursSpecification, no FoundingDate, no reviews block. The legacy story is told only on /about and is not machine-tagged.

competitors: El Mexicali Café II (Indio, named USA Today 2026 Restaurant of the Year)
relationships.corridor_role: anchor — one of the oldest continuously operating Mexican restaurants in Indio (1964), pre-dating El Tranvia (1969) and Jalisco (1980); functions as a legacy reference point for Indio Blvd south of the I-10
query_wins: Editorial citation queries via Visit Greater Palm Springs and Palm Springs Life round-ups

convertibility.marketing_spend_signal: Real custom-built site (Limon Media credit), active Facebook and Instagram, paid/claimed Tripadvisor and Yelp business listings, Birdeye review-management presence (315 reviews aggregated suggests an active review tool subscription). No agency footprint visible beyond Limon Media (web). Modest but real ongoing marketing investment.
convertibility.reasoning: Independently owned by the Flores family for 62 years with a reachable owner-operator footprint (named family members managing day-to-day, single location, locally hosted site). Evidence of real marketing spend (custom domain and site, Birdeye review platform, active social, paid directory presences) plus a high reputation-to-visibility gap make this an unusually strong fit for a category-map conversion play. The Press page is built but empty — they've architected the slot and not filled it. Clear convertible target.

Google review count not directly verified; ~4.4 rating is sourced from a third-party aggregator (Restaurant Guru) [UNVERIFIED]
Restaurant Guru's ~1,488 review aggregate appears to combine multiple platforms and may double-count [UNVERIFIED]

No verified third-party major awards (no Michelin, no James Beard, no confirmed Desert Sun Best of, no confirmed Palm Springs Life 'Best Of' win) — only editorial round-up inclusions, which are not awards [UNVERIFIED whether any Best-Of category win exists]
Palm Springs Life Indio culinary guide returned 403 — could not directly confirm Rincón Norteño is named in that specific article
Founder origins (Prudencio from Austin TX, Estella from Santa Rita NM, relocated to Indio 1959) sourced from restaurant's own About page only — not independently corroborated [UNVERIFIED]

content.text: Submitted. Key findings: Rincón Norteño is a 62-year multi-generational Indio anchor with steady, mostly-positive reviews across multiple platforms but a sizable visibility gap — it does not surface in generative regional queries even for breakfast dishes it literally serves, its own /press page is built-but-empty, and the site carries zero schema.org markup despite clean text-level NAP and hours. Independent ownership, real (modest) marketing spend (custom site via Limon Media, Birdeye, active social) make this a clear "convertible" tier rather than anchor-reference. Visibility gap scored 8/10.
